MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES
Off Calendar Aug-16-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Maddona Herman for plaintiff and moving party
Kenneth N. frucht for defendant and opposing party
HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted with modifications: 4) Special interrogatories
13, 14, 15, 16
Granted. Defendants are ordered to respond further with factual information that supports the contentions. The Court notes that defendant identifies the same two documents to support the contentions described in interrogatories #13 and 15.
23, 47, 48
Granted. Defendants are ordered to respond further based on currently available information, whether or not a full response is capable of being made at this time.
24
Granted. Defendants are ordered to respond further. Stating that 'all documents' relate to the contention in interrogatory #23 is non-responsive since no documents are identified with any reasonable degree of particularity.
55
Granted. The response is non-responsive. Defendants are ordered to identify the amount of the accounts payable that have been paid.
56
Granted. Defendant is ordered to conduct a reasonable and diligent search to determine if any documents other than the cancelled checks evidence the specified payments.

Further responses are to be provided within 15 days of notice of this order. The Court finds that defendants made evasive responses to discovery. The Court finds that adequate notice of the request for sanctions was given in the memorandum of points and authorities' table of contents, introduction, section E of the legal argument, and the conclusion. Accordingly, monetary sanctions in the amount of $2136.30 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order.
Modification: That sanctions are to be paid in 60 days. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES
Off Calendar Aug-16-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Maddona Herman for plaintiff and moving party
Kenneth N. frucht for defendant and opposing party
HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted:
3) Request for production
19, 22
The unspecified objection is overruled and stricken. Defendants are ordered to respond. If there are no responsive documents, the answer shall comply with CCP 2031.230.
COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES
Off Calendar Aug-16-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Maddona Herman for plaintiff and moving party
Kenneth N. frucht for defendant and opposing party
HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted:
1) Form interrogatories:
Unless otherwise noted, the rulings as to form interrogatories apply to both Harold and Judith Ticktin.
8.7, 8.8
Granted. Defendants are ordered to respond further based on currently available information, whether or not a precise amount is capable of calculation.
9.1
Granted. Defendants are ordered to respond to all subparts.
15.1
Granted. Defendants are ordered to respond. It is insufficient to state that discovery is ongoing.
17.1
Request for admissions 11, 12, 13, 23, 25 (Harold), 26 (Harold)
Granted. The responses are incomplete in that there are no facts stated, merely conclusions, and no documents are identified with any reasonable degree of particularity relating to subpart (d). Defendants are ordered to respond further.
Request for admissions 14, 15, 16, 17
Granted. The objections are stricken. Defendants are ordered to respond and may define the term balance in the answers.
Request for admissions 25, 26 (Judith)
Granted. Defendant is ordered to respond. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES

2) Request for admission
15
